bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues are: issueA=valueF (1.0), issueB=valueF (1.0), issueC=valueF (1.0), issueD=valueF (1.0). This gives me maximum utility of 1.0. Early in the negotiation, I should anchor high.

We're very early (t=0.02). The opponent responded with all valueA (minimum for me) and complained about my opening offer being too self-serving. Their message is aggressive but it's cheap talk - their actual bid is equally extreme in their favor. I should concede slightly from my opening max-utility bid to show willingness to negotiate, but stay high since we're still very early. My high-weight issues are issueD and issueC (0.3 each), so I'll hold firm on those. I can give a small concession on issueA (low weight 0.1) and issueB (weight 0.2).

The opponent is consistently bidding all-valueA (their best, my worst) and using aggressive language, but it's very early (t=0.04). Their behavior suggests they're anchoring hard. I should make a moderate concession from my previous bid, particularly on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB), while holding firm on my high-weight issues (issueC, issueD) where I have most utility. I'll concede issueA and issueB a bit more while keeping issueC and issueD high.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all-A values (minimum utility for me) every single time, while complaining loudly about my offers. This is very early (t=0.06), so I don't need to make major concessions yet. Their messages are aggressive but their bidding behavior shows no movement whatsoever - they're holding firm at their best position. I should continue conceding moderately on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB) while holding strong on my high-weight issues (issueC, issueD). I'll try conceding a bit more on issueA and issueB since they have lower importance to me, while keeping issueC at valueF and issueD at valueE or better.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-valueA (minimum utility for me) while aggressively complaining about my offers. Their behavior shows no actual concession - they're just repeating the same extreme bid. At t=0.08 we're still very early, so I should not cave to their pressure. I've been conceding on issueA and issueB (low importance to me) while holding firm on issueC and issueD (high importance, 0.3 each). I'll continue this strategy - making small concessions on lower-priority issues while protecting my high-value issues. I'll move issueB to valueB and keep issueC at valueF and issueD at valueE to maintain good utility while showing some movement.

The opponent has been stubbornly offering all valueA across every round, which is their worst possible offer for me. Their aggressive messages are cheap talk - they claim I haven't conceded enough but they haven't moved at all themselves. At t=0.10 we're still early, so I don't need to capitulate. I'll hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueC and issueD) while slightly conceding on lower-weight issues. My previous attempt had a formatting error - I need to fix the JSON structure with proper separate keys.
